sections.paragraph:
to cut, to cut up, to divide out, i.q. Arab. حبر. It occurs once Isa 47:13 קרי, הֹבְרֵי שָׁמַיִם “those who divide the heavens,” for purposes of augury, taking a horoscope, i.e. augers, astrologers; LXX. ἀστρολόγοι τοῦ οὐρανοῦ. Vulg. augures cœli. כתיב, הָבְרוּ שׁ׳ (אֲשֶׁר). See my Comment. on Isa. ii. 351, seq. Others take הָבַר as i.q. غبر to know; while others would read חֹבְרֵי, comparing חֲבָרַיִךְ ver. 10 Isa 47:10.

[“הֵגֵא Est 2:3, and הֵגַי verses , 15 (Hege, Hegai), pr.n. of a eunuch in the court of Xerxes.” Thes. “Benfey compares âgà, eunuch; Monats-namen, page 192.”]
